domains.name: Python Programming
domains.description: Core Python language concepts from basics to advanced patterns

domains.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
domains.description: Fundamental data structures, algorithms, and complexity analysis

domains.name: Web Development
domains.description: Backend web development concepts including HTTP, APIs, and databases

domains.name: Frontend Development
domains.description: Client-side web development with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and React

domains.name: DevOps & Infrastructure
domains.description: CI/CD, containers, orchestration, cloud, and infrastructure automation

domains.name: Databases
domains.description: Relational and NoSQL databases, SQL, indexing, and data management

domains.name: Machine Learning & AI
domains.description: Statistics, ML algorithms, deep learning, and model deployment

domains.name: System Design
domains.description: Scalability, distributed systems, caching, and architecture patterns

domains.name: Cybersecurity
domains.description: Application security, cryptography, network security, and auth

domains.name: Git & Version Control
domains.description: Version control workflows, branching strategies, and collaboration

concepts.name: Variables & Types
concepts.description: Variable assignment, naming conventions, and Python's type system including int, float, str, bool, and None.

transfers_to.description: Variable concepts transfer to JS DOM variables

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks variables are containers rather than names referencing objects.
indicators: Confused when two variables point to same mutable object
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Assign a list to two variables, mutate through one

concepts.name: Data Types & Structures
concepts.description: Built-in types: lists, tuples, dicts, sets. Mutability, iteration, and common operations.

common_misconceptions.description: Confuses mutable and immutable types.
indicators: Doesn't understand why dict keys must be hashable
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Ask what happens when you try tuple[0] = 5

concepts.name: Control Flow
concepts.description: If/elif/else conditionals, boolean logic, and conditional expressions.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses multiple if when elif is needed.
indicators: Multiple if blocks that should be exclusive
Surprised when more than one branch executes
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Grade classifier with overlapping if/if/if

concepts.name: Loops & Iteration
concepts.description: For loops, while loops, break/continue, range(), enumerate(), and zip().

transfers_to.description: Loops are essential for array traversal

common_misconceptions.description: Off by one errors in loop boundaries.
indicators: Uses range(1,n) when range(n) is correct
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Print numbers 1 to 10 using range()

concepts.name: Functions
concepts.description: Defining functions, parameters, return values, default arguments, *args/**kwargs.

transfers_to.description: Functions as building blocks for middleware chains

common_misconceptions.description: Confuses return with print.
indicators: Uses print inside function expecting caller to get value
Functions that print instead of returning
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Write function that returns sum, then try to use result

concepts.name: Scope & Namespaces
concepts.description: LEGB rule, local vs global scope, nonlocal keyword, and namespace resolution.

common_misconceptions.description: Doesn't understand when global keyword is needed.
indicators: Tries to modify global variable without global keyword
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Counter function that modifies a global variable

concepts.name: String Methods & Formatting
concepts.description: String manipulation: split, join, strip, replace, f-strings, and regex basics.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks strings can be modified in place.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Try to change first character of a string

concepts.name: Classes & Objects
concepts.description: Class definitions, __init__, instance methods, attributes, and encapsulation.

transfers_to.description: Object state management parallels component state

common_misconceptions.description: Confuses class attributes with instance attributes.
indicators: Modifies class attribute expecting only one instance to change
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Class with shared list attribute vs instance list

concepts.name: Inheritance & Polymorphism
concepts.description: Single/multiple inheritance, super(), method resolution order, abstract base classes.

common_misconceptions.description: Doesn't understand MRO in multiple inheritance.
indicators: Unexpected method called in diamond hierarchy
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Diamond inheritance with conflicting methods

concepts.name: Exception Handling
concepts.description: Try/except/else/finally, raising exceptions, custom exception classes, and error hierarchies.

transfers_to.description: Exception handling is foundation of web error handling

common_misconceptions.description: Uses bare except catching everything including SystemExit.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Debug a program where KeyboardInterrupt is caught accidentally

concepts.name: File I/O
concepts.description: Reading/writing files, context managers for files, CSV/JSON handling, and path operations.

common_misconceptions.description: Forgets to close files or doesn't use context managers.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Rewrite file operations using with statement

concepts.name: Modules & Packages
concepts.description: Import system, creating modules, __init__.py, relative imports, and virtual environments.

common_misconceptions.description: Creates circular import dependencies.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Refactor circular imports between two modules

concepts.name: List Comprehensions
concepts.description: List, dict, and set comprehensions, generator expressions, and nested comprehensions.

common_misconceptions.description: Writes unreadable nested comprehensions instead of loops.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Refactor complex comprehension into readable loop

concepts.name: Closures & First-Class Functions
concepts.description: Functions as objects, closures, higher-order functions, map/filter/reduce.

common_misconceptions.description: Doesn't understand late binding in closures over loop variables.
indicators: Lambda in loop captures last value
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Create list of lambdas in a loop and call each

concepts.name: Decorators
concepts.description: Function and class decorators, @syntax, functools.wraps, parameterized decorators.

transfers_to.description: Decorators are conceptually similar to middleware wrapping

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks decorator runs when decorated function is called, not at definition.
indicators: Surprised by side effects at import time
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Add print to decorator and show it runs at definition

concepts.name: Generators & Iterators
concepts.description: Yield keyword, generator functions, iterator protocol, lazy evaluation, and itertools.

common_misconceptions.description: Doesn't understand generators are single-use.
indicators: Iterates generator twice expecting same results
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Iterate a generator twice and explain the empty second pass

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.description: Recursive functions, base cases, call stack, tail recursion, and memoization.

transfers_to.description: Recursion is fundamental to tree algorithms

common_misconceptions.description: Forgets base case causing infinite recursion.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Add base case to broken recursive function

concepts.name: Async Programming
concepts.description: async/await, asyncio event loop, coroutines, Tasks, and concurrent execution.

transfers_to.description: Async is required for WebSocket handling

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks async means parallel/multi-threaded execution.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Explain async as single chef multitasking vs multiple chefs

concepts.name: Context Managers
concepts.description: with statement, __enter__/__exit__, contextlib, and resource management patterns.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks context managers are only for file handling.
indicators: Never uses with for DB connections or locks
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Build a database connection context manager

concepts.name: Type Hints & Annotations
concepts.description: Type annotations, typing module, Optional, Union, generics, and mypy.

transfers_to.description: Type hints serve similar purpose as TypeScript types

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks type hints enforce types at runtime.
indicators: Expects TypeError from wrong type passed
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Pass wrong type to annotated function and show it still runs

concepts.name: Testing & pytest
concepts.description: Unit testing with pytest, fixtures, mocking, parameterized tests, and test-driven development.

common_misconceptions.description: Tests implementation details instead of behavior.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Refactor code and show behavior tests still pass

concepts.name: Virtual Environments & Packaging
concepts.description: venv, pip, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, and dependency management.

common_misconceptions.description: Installs packages globally instead of in virtual environments.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Create venv, install package, show isolation

concepts.name: Arrays & Lists
concepts.description: Contiguous and dynamic arrays, indexing, slicing, time complexity of operations.

transfers_to.description: Array indexing concepts apply to database indexes

common_misconceptions.description: Doesn't know insert/delete is O(n) for arrays.
indicators: Unaware of deque for front operations
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Benchmark list.insert(0) vs deque.appendleft()

concepts.name: Linked Lists
concepts.description: Singly and doubly linked lists, node-based structure, insertion, deletion, traversal.

common_misconceptions.description: Tries to index a linked list like an array.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Compare linked list to treasure hunt vs array to numbered lockers

concepts.name: Stacks
concepts.description: LIFO structure, push/pop operations, stack-based algorithms, and call stack.

transfers_to.description: Stack frames underpin recursive calls

common_misconceptions.description: Mixes up LIFO and FIFO behavior.
indicators: Uses stack where queue is needed
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Trace push/pop vs enqueue/dequeue with same input

concepts.name: Queues
concepts.description: FIFO structure, enqueue/dequeue, priority queues, and deque.

transfers_to.description: Queue data structure concepts underpin message queue systems

common_misconceptions.description: Confused about which end to add/remove from.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Animate enqueue/dequeue operations step by step

concepts.name: Hash Maps & Sets
concepts.description: Hash functions, collision resolution, hash tables, Python dicts, time complexity.

transfers_to.description: Hash indexes in databases use same principles

common_misconceptions.description: Assumes hash operations are always O(1).
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Demo hash collision chain with bad hash function

concepts.name: Trees & Binary Trees
concepts.description: Tree terminology, binary trees, BST, insertion, deletion, and balanced trees.

common_misconceptions.description: Doesn't understand trees are acyclic connected graphs.
indicators: Doesn't check for cycle in tree problems
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Family tree vs social network graph comparison

concepts.name: Tree Traversal
concepts.description: Inorder, preorder, postorder, level-order traversal, DFS and BFS on trees.

common_misconceptions.description: Mixes up pre/in/post order.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Trace all three traversals on same tree side by side

concepts.name: Graphs
concepts.description: Graph representations (adjacency list/matrix), directed/undirected, weighted graphs, and traversal.

common_misconceptions.description: Only knows adjacency matrix, not adjacency list.
indicators: Uses matrix for sparse graphs wasting space
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Implement same graph with both representations and compare

concepts.name: Sorting Algorithms
concepts.description: Bubble, selection, insertion, merge, quick sort. Time/space complexity comparisons.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses same sort for all cases without considering data characteristics.
indicators: Always uses quicksort ignoring worst case
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show quicksort O(n^2) on sorted input vs mergesort O(n log n)

concepts.name: Searching Algorithms
concepts.description: Linear search, binary search, search in sorted/unsorted data, and search space reduction.

common_misconceptions.description: Applies binary search to unsorted data.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Run binary search on unsorted array and trace the failure

concepts.name: Heaps & Priority Queues
concepts.description: Min/max heaps, heapify, heap operations, and Python heapq module.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks a heap is a fully sorted structure.
indicators: Expects sorted order from heap iteration
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show heap array representation vs sorted array

concepts.name: Tries
concepts.description: Prefix trees, insertion, search, autocomplete, and space optimization.

common_misconceptions.description: Doesn't know when trie beats hash map for string lookups.
indicators: Uses hash map for prefix search
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Implement autocomplete with trie vs hash map and compare

concepts.name: Dynamic Programming
concepts.description: Memoization, tabulation, optimal substructure, overlapping subproblems.

common_misconceptions.description: Can't identify when DP applies vs plain recursion.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Draw recursion tree for fibonacci showing repeated calls

concepts.name: Graph Algorithms
concepts.description: Dijkstra, BFS/DFS applications, topological sort, minimum spanning trees.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses Dijkstra with negative edge weights.
indicators: Wrong shortest paths with negative edges
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show Dijkstra failure on negative weight graph

concepts.name: Complexity Analysis
concepts.description: Big-O, Big-Omega, Big-Theta, amortized analysis, space vs time tradeoffs.

common_misconceptions.description: Ignores constants and lower-order terms when they matter in practice.
indicators: Claims O(n) is always faster than O(n log n)
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Benchmark O(n) with huge constant vs O(n log n) with small constant

concepts.name: HTTP Protocol
concepts.description: HTTP methods, status codes, headers, request/response cycle, HTTPS, and HTTP/2.

transfers_to.description: Understanding HTTP helps with fetch/AJAX calls

common_misconceptions.description: Uses GET for data mutation or POST for idempotent reads.
indicators: Sends passwords in GET query strings
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Design a REST API showing correct method usage

concepts.name: REST API Design
concepts.description: RESTful principles, resource naming, CRUD operations, status codes, and versioning.

transfers_to.description: REST and GraphQL are alternative API paradigms

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks REST is just returning JSON over HTTP.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Refactor a non-RESTful API to follow REST conventions

concepts.name: Request-Response Cycle
concepts.description: Client-server model, request parsing, response formatting, serialization, and content negotiation.

common_misconceptions.description: Assumes all requests are synchronous.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Restaurant order: waiter doesn't wait at kitchen

concepts.name: Authentication & Authorization
concepts.description: Sessions, tokens, JWT, OAuth2, password hashing, and role-based access control.

transfers_to.description: Auth concepts directly relate to JWT security

common_misconceptions.description: Confuses authentication with authorization.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Airport: ID check (authn) vs boarding pass check (authz)

concepts.name: Web Error Handling
concepts.description: Error responses, exception middleware, validation errors, and graceful degradation.

common_misconceptions.description: Returns 500 for all errors instead of proper status codes.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Add proper error handling to a bare FastAPI endpoint

concepts.name: Database Integration
concepts.description: Connecting to databases from web apps, ORMs, raw SQL, connection pooling, and migrations.

transfers_to.description: Web DB integration relies on ORM patterns

common_misconceptions.description: Triggers N+1 query problem without realizing.
indicators: Massive query counts for simple pages
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Profile a page that loads users then loops to load each user's posts

concepts.name: Middleware
concepts.description: Request/response middleware, middleware chains, logging, auth, and CORS middleware.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks middleware execution order doesn't matter.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Trace request through middleware chain showing order dependency

concepts.name: API Design Patterns
concepts.description: Pagination, filtering, versioning, HATEOAS, idempotency, and documentation.

common_misconceptions.description: Returns entire dataset in single response.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Add cursor-based pagination to an endpoint returning 10K records

concepts.name: WebSockets
concepts.description: Full-duplex communication, WebSocket protocol, Socket.IO, and real-time patterns.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses WebSockets when simple polling or SSE would suffice.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Compare WebSocket vs SSE vs polling for a notification system

concepts.name: GraphQL
concepts.description: Schema definition, queries, mutations, resolvers, and N+1 problem in GraphQL.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks GraphQL should replace REST everywhere.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Compare REST vs GraphQL for simple vs complex data needs

concepts.name: Web Caching
concepts.description: HTTP caching headers, CDN caching, server-side caching, cache invalidation strategies.

transfers_to.description: Web caching fundamentals apply to system-level caching

common_misconceptions.description: Caches everything without considering invalidation.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Debug a system serving stale user profile after update

concepts.name: CORS
concepts.description: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing, preflight requests, allowed origins/methods/headers.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * in production.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show why * fails when cookies/auth are needed

concepts.name: HTML Fundamentals
concepts.description: Semantic HTML, document structure, forms, tables, accessibility, and SEO basics.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses div for everything instead of semantic elements.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Refactor a div-soup page to use semantic HTML

concepts.name: CSS Fundamentals
concepts.description: Selectors, box model, specificity, cascade, positioning, and responsive units.

common_misconceptions.description: Overuses !important instead of understanding specificity.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Calculate specificity scores for competing selectors

concepts.name: CSS Flexbox
concepts.description: Flex container/items, main/cross axis, justify-content, align-items, and flex-wrap.

common_misconceptions.description: Confuses main axis and cross axis.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Diagram main vs cross axis for row and column directions

concepts.name: CSS Grid
concepts.description: Grid container, template rows/columns, grid areas, auto-fill/fit, and gap.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses grid for 1D layouts and flexbox for 2D.
indicators: Grid for simple row of items
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Build same layout with grid and flexbox, compare when each is better

concepts.name: DOM Manipulation
concepts.description: Selecting elements, modifying content/styles, creating/removing nodes, and the DOM tree.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses innerHTML with user input creating XSS vulnerability.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show XSS attack through innerHTML and fix with textContent

concepts.name: Event Handling
concepts.description: Event listeners, event propagation (bubbling/capturing), delegation, and preventDefault.

common_misconceptions.description: Attaches listeners to every child instead of delegating.
indicators: Hundreds of listeners on list items
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Refactor individual li listeners to single ul delegation

concepts.name: Responsive Design
concepts.description: Media queries, mobile-first design, viewport meta, and responsive images.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses fixed pixel breakpoints instead of content-based.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Resize browser and find where layout breaks to set breakpoints

concepts.name: React Fundamentals
concepts.description: Components, JSX, props, rendering, and component composition.

common_misconceptions.description: Tries to modify props directly in child component.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show prop immutability and lift state up pattern

concepts.name: React State Management
concepts.description: useState, lifting state, derived state, controlled components, and state patterns.

common_misconceptions.description: Mutates state directly instead of using setter.
indicators: Object/array state changes don't trigger re-render
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show arr.push vs setArr([...arr, item]) behavior difference

concepts.name: React Hooks
concepts.description: useEffect, useContext, useRef, useMemo, useCallback, and custom hooks.

common_misconceptions.description: Forgets dependency array causing infinite re-renders.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Add missing dependency array to fix infinite fetch loop

concepts.name: TypeScript
concepts.description: Type system, interfaces, generics, type guards, and TypeScript with React.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses 'any' type to bypass type checking.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Remove all 'any' types from a component and add proper types

concepts.name: Build Tools & Bundlers
concepts.description: Vite, Webpack, bundling, code splitting, tree shaking, and dev servers.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks bundler is part of the framework.
indicators: Confused when changing from CRA to Vite
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Set up a React app with Vite from scratch showing each build step

concepts.name: Linux Fundamentals
concepts.description: File system, permissions, processes, package management, and common commands.

common_misconceptions.description: Runs everything as root instead of using proper permissions.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Set up proper user permissions for a web application

concepts.name: Shell Scripting
concepts.description: Bash scripts, variables, conditionals, loops, pipes, and automation.

common_misconceptions.description: Shell scripts without error handling or set -e.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Add set -euo pipefail and trap to a fragile script

concepts.name: Docker
concepts.description: Images, containers, Dockerfile, layers, volumes, networking, and multi-stage builds.

transfers_to.description: Docker is prerequisite for Kubernetes

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks containers are lightweight VMs.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Container is a process with isolated namespace, not a full OS

concepts.name: Docker Compose
concepts.description: Multi-container orchestration, services, networks, volumes, and compose files.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses docker-compose in production without orchestrator.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Add health checks and restart policies to compose file

concepts.name: Kubernetes
concepts.description: Pods, deployments, services, ingress, ConfigMaps, and container orchestration at scale.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses Kubernetes for simple apps that don't need it.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Decision matrix: when Docker Compose vs K8s is appropriate

concepts.name: CI/CD Pipelines
concepts.description: Continuous integration, continuous deployment, pipeline stages, and automated testing.

transfers_to.description: GitHub Actions is a primary CI/CD implementation

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks CI and CD are the same thing.
indicators: No distinction between integration and deployment
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: CI = building and testing the car, CD = delivering it to customer

concepts.name: GitHub Actions
concepts.description: Workflows, triggers, jobs, steps, actions marketplace, and secrets management.

common_misconceptions.description: Hardcodes secrets in workflow files.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Move hardcoded secrets to GitHub repository secrets

concepts.name: Terraform & IaC
concepts.description: Infrastructure as code, HCL syntax, providers, state management, and modules.

common_misconceptions.description: Manually edits terraform.tfstate file.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Use terraform import and state mv instead of manual edits

concepts.name: Monitoring & Observability
concepts.description: Metrics, logging, tracing, alerting, Prometheus, Grafana, and SLOs.

common_misconceptions.description: Only monitors uptime, not latency, errors, or saturation.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Set up RED metrics: Rate, Errors, Duration for an API

concepts.name: Cloud Computing Fundamentals
concepts.description: Cloud service models (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS), AWS/GCP/Azure basics, regions, and core services.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks cloud is just renting servers.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Cloud is a utility like electricity - you don't build a power plant

concepts.name: SQL Fundamentals
concepts.description: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, WHERE, ORDER BY, GROUP BY, and aggregate functions.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses SELECT * in production queries.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show performance difference between SELECT * and specific columns

concepts.name: SQL Joins
concepts.description: INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL joins, self-joins, and join performance.

common_misconceptions.description: Confuses LEFT JOIN with INNER JOIN behavior.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Venn diagram of join types with sample data showing differences

concepts.name: Database Indexes
concepts.description: B-tree indexes, composite indexes, index selection, and query plan analysis.

common_misconceptions.description: Adds indexes to every column without considering write overhead.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Use EXPLAIN to show query plan before and after adding targeted index

concepts.name: Database Normalization
concepts.description: Normal forms (1NF-3NF), denormalization tradeoffs, and schema design.

common_misconceptions.description: Normalizes everything without considering read performance.
indicators: Too many joins for simple reads
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Design schema for read-heavy vs write-heavy workload

concepts.name: Transactions & ACID
concepts.description: ACID properties, isolation levels, deadlocks, and transaction management.

transfers_to.description: ACID understanding is prerequisite for CAP theorem

common_misconceptions.description: Uses default isolation without understanding implications.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Demo dirty read at READ UNCOMMITTED vs SERIALIZABLE

concepts.name: NoSQL Databases
concepts.description: Document stores, key-value, column-family, graph databases, and when to use each.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks NoSQL means no schema at all.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show schema validation in MongoDB and consistent document patterns

concepts.name: Redis
concepts.description: In-memory data store, data types, pub/sub, caching patterns, and persistence.

transfers_to.description: Redis is a primary web caching technology

common_misconceptions.description: Uses Redis as primary database without persistence strategy.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Configure Redis RDB/AOF persistence and test recovery

concepts.name: ORMs
concepts.description: Object-relational mapping, SQLAlchemy, query building, relationships, and migrations.

common_misconceptions.description: Relies entirely on ORM without understanding generated SQL.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Enable SQL logging and find N+1 query in ORM code

concepts.name: Query Optimization
concepts.description: EXPLAIN plans, query profiling, index usage, and rewriting slow queries.

common_misconceptions.description: Optimizes queries without looking at execution plan.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to identify actual bottleneck in slow query

concepts.name: Database Replication
concepts.description: Primary-replica setup, synchronous/async replication, failover, and read replicas.

transfers_to.description: Replication is a key scalability technique

common_misconceptions.description: Doesn't account for replication lag in read-after-write.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Demo read-after-write inconsistency with async replica

concepts.name: Linear Algebra for ML
concepts.description: Vectors, matrices, matrix operations, eigenvalues, and their role in ML algorithms.

common_misconceptions.description: Confuses matrix multiplication with element-wise multiplication.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Side-by-side comparison of np.dot vs * with matrices

concepts.name: Statistics & Probability
concepts.description: Distributions, hypothesis testing, Bayes theorem, correlation, and statistical significance.

common_misconceptions.description: Assumes correlation implies causation.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show spurious correlations dataset and discuss confounders

concepts.name: Supervised Learning
concepts.description: Regression, classification, training/test split, cross-validation, and common algorithms.

common_misconceptions.description: Evaluates model on training data.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show model with 99% train accuracy and 60% test accuracy

concepts.name: Unsupervised Learning
concepts.description: Clustering, dimensionality reduction, anomaly detection, and association rules.

common_misconceptions.description: Picks arbitrary K without using elbow method or silhouette.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Use elbow method and silhouette scores to find optimal K

concepts.name: Neural Networks
concepts.description: Perceptrons, layers, activation functions, backpropagation, and gradient descent.

common_misconceptions.description: Assumes more layers always improve performance.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Train shallow vs deep network on small dataset showing overfitting

concepts.name: Deep Learning
concepts.description: CNNs, RNNs, transformers, transfer learning, and modern architectures.

common_misconceptions.description: Trains large models from scratch instead of fine-tuning.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Compare from-scratch vs fine-tuned model on small dataset

concepts.name: Natural Language Processing
concepts.description: Tokenization, embeddings, language models, text classification, and NER.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks bag-of-words is sufficient for all text tasks.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Compare BOW vs embedding-based model on sentiment analysis

concepts.name: Computer Vision
concepts.description: Image preprocessing, CNNs for vision, object detection, and image segmentation.

common_misconceptions.description: Trains vision models without data augmentation.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show accuracy improvement from adding rotation/flip augmentation

concepts.name: Model Evaluation
concepts.description: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, confusion matrix, and bias-variance tradeoff.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses accuracy on imbalanced datasets.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show model with 99% accuracy that never predicts minority class

concepts.name: Feature Engineering
concepts.description: Feature selection, encoding categoricals, scaling, creating features, and handling missing data.

common_misconceptions.description: Feeds raw features without any preprocessing.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show model improvement from proper scaling and encoding

concepts.name: Scalability
concepts.description: Horizontal vs vertical scaling, stateless design, and bottleneck identification.

common_misconceptions.description: Only considers vertical scaling.
indicators: Keeps upgrading server instead of distributing
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Design stateless service that scales horizontally

concepts.name: Load Balancing
concepts.description: Round-robin, least connections, consistent hashing, health checks, and L4/L7 balancing.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses sticky sessions preventing true horizontal scaling.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Migrate from sticky sessions to stateless + external session store

concepts.name: Caching Strategies
concepts.description: Cache-aside, write-through, write-behind, TTL, eviction policies, and cache invalidation.

common_misconceptions.description: No invalidation strategy leading to stale data.
indicators: Users see old data after updates
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Debug a system where user profile update doesn't show immediately

concepts.name: Message Queues
concepts.description: Producer-consumer pattern, RabbitMQ, Kafka, at-least-once delivery, and dead letter queues.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses message queue as a database for long-term storage.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Queue is a conveyor belt, not a warehouse

concepts.name: Microservices
concepts.description: Service decomposition, inter-service communication, data ownership, and distributed tracing.

common_misconceptions.description: Starts with microservices instead of monolith.
indicators: Distributed system complexity on day one
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show monolith-first approach and when/how to extract services

concepts.name: API Gateway
concepts.description: Request routing, rate limiting, authentication, aggregation, and gateway patterns.

common_misconceptions.description: Puts too much business logic in the gateway.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Refactor business logic from gateway into proper services

concepts.name: Database Sharding
concepts.description: Horizontal partitioning, shard keys, consistent hashing, and resharding strategies.

common_misconceptions.description: Shards database before exhausting simpler optimizations.
indicators: Should have used read replicas first
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Decision tree: indexes → read replicas → caching → sharding

concepts.name: CAP Theorem
concepts.description: Consistency, availability, partition tolerance tradeoffs in distributed systems.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks you literally pick exactly two of three.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show how systems like DynamoDB and Spanner make nuanced tradeoffs

concepts.name: Rate Limiting
concepts.description: Token bucket, sliding window, fixed window algorithms, and distributed rate limiting.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses fixed window allowing burst at window boundaries.
indicators: Double the rate at window edges
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show request burst at fixed window boundary vs sliding window

concepts.name: Content Delivery Networks
concepts.description: Edge caching, CDN architecture, cache purging, and static asset delivery.

common_misconceptions.description: Caches dynamic/personalized content on CDN.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Configure CDN rules for static vs dynamic content separation

concepts.name: OWASP Top 10
concepts.description: Top web application security risks: injection, broken auth, XSS, CSRF, misconfiguration, etc.

common_misconceptions.description: Treats security as a nice-to-have, not a requirement.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Walk through OWASP checklist on a sample application

concepts.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
concepts.description: Stored, reflected, DOM-based XSS, sanitization, CSP, and prevention techniques.

common_misconceptions.description: Only escapes HTML but not JavaScript contexts or attributes.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show XSS payload that bypasses HTML-only escaping

concepts.name: SQL Injection
concepts.description: SQL injection techniques, parameterized queries, ORM safety, and detection.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks using an ORM makes SQL injection impossible.
indicators: Raw queries in ORM still vulnerable
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show SQL injection through ORM raw() method

concepts.name: Cross-Site Request Forgery
concepts.description: CSRF attacks, token-based prevention, SameSite cookies, and double-submit pattern.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks CSRF only affects POST requests.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Demo CSRF attack via img tag on unprotected endpoint

concepts.name: Encryption
concepts.description: Symmetric/asymmetric encryption, TLS, key management, and encryption at rest.

common_misconceptions.description: Confuses encryption with encoding (Base64).
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Decode Base64 password and show it's not encryption

concepts.name: Hashing & Password Storage
concepts.description: Hash functions, bcrypt, salt, password storage best practices, and integrity checks.

common_misconceptions.description: Uses MD5 or SHA for password hashing.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Show bcrypt vs MD5 brute-force time comparison

concepts.name: JWT Authentication
concepts.description: JWT structure, signing, validation, refresh tokens, and common JWT vulnerabilities.

common_misconceptions.description: Stores sensitive data in JWT payload thinking it's encrypted.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Decode JWT on jwt.io and show payload is just base64

concepts.name: Network Security
concepts.description: Firewalls, VPNs, network segmentation, port scanning, and defense in depth.

common_misconceptions.description: Only secures the network perimeter, not internal traffic.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Design defense-in-depth with network segmentation and zero trust

concepts.name: Git Fundamentals
concepts.description: Init, add, commit, status, log, diff, and the staging area concept.

common_misconceptions.description: Thinks git add and git commit are the same step.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Trace file through working dir → staging → repository

concepts.name: Branching
concepts.description: Creating branches, switching, branch naming, and branch-based workflows.

common_misconceptions.description: Commits directly to main instead of feature branches.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Create feature branch, make changes, merge back to main

concepts.name: Merging & Conflict Resolution
concepts.description: Fast-forward merge, three-way merge, conflict resolution, and merge strategies.

common_misconceptions.description: Panics at merge conflicts and abandons merge.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Walk through a simple conflict resolution step by step

concepts.name: Rebasing
concepts.description: Interactive rebase, rebase vs merge, squashing commits, and rewriting history.

common_misconceptions.description: Rebases shared/public branches causing history divergence.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Rebase is rewriting history - never rewrite published history

concepts.name: Pull Requests & Code Review
concepts.description: PR workflow, code review practices, review comments, and CI integration.

common_misconceptions.description: Creates massive PRs that are impossible to review.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Break a large changeset into small, reviewable PRs

concepts.name: Git Flow & Branching Strategies
concepts.description: Git Flow, GitHub Flow, trunk-based development, and release management.

common_misconceptions.description: Applies Git Flow to small projects that need trunk-based.
common_misconceptions.example_trigger: Compare Git Flow vs trunk-based for small team vs enterprise
